workflows.derivation: ci.yml is the only checked-in workflow with a pull_request trigger. Its jobs code-quality, security-scan, tests, build-release, and publish are PR-reachable, but none of their run steps carry a direct doc-lattice marker (D2 raw marker facts empty on every PR-reachable body and template). The only marker-bearing execution sources (the complex Bash, steps 4/7/10) live in the release job, which is push-only and pruned by D1 for the pull_request event. With no PR-reachable marker-bearing source, D6 batches nothing: marker_gated_sources and batched_sources are empty, and the audit yields zero findings and zero diagnostics.

derivation: Tier 2 enumerates every checked-in workflow carrying a PR trigger. Exactly one qualifies (.github/workflows/ci.yml). Applying D1 PR-reachability, D2 raw marker facts, and the D6 composition table, every PR-reachable step is marker-free and the sole marker-bearing job (release) is pruned as push-only, so the repository audit is clean: zero findings, zero diagnostics. This matches the current clean ci audit; no zeros were forced.
